name: Enchiladas South Texas Style
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt shortening in a heavy skillet.
2. Add the ground beef and brown it.
3. Sprinkle meat with flour; mix in the skillet.
4. Add tomato sauce and 1 cup water.
5. Mix half a cup of water with chili powder to form a smooth paste; add to the meat mixture.
6. Add salt to taste and garlic powder.
7. Cook over medium heat, uncovered, until it reaches gravy consistency.
8. Cover and simmer on very low heat. Add more water if the chili gravy becomes too thick.
9. Dip tortillas one at a time in the hot chili gravy using a wide metal spatula. The tortillas will become pliable quickly, making rolling easier. Soaking for too long may cause them to fall apart.
10. Place cheese and onion on one side of the tortilla near the center.
11. Roll the tortilla tightly around the filling, placing the loose side down in a glass casserole baking dish.
12. For best results, place enchiladas in a row with sides touching.
13. Pour remaining hot chili gravy over all enchiladas and sprinkle generously with grated cheese and chopped onion.
14. Bake at 350°F until the cheese is melted. Serve immediately.
